task_completion.description: Open NASA Panoply and load the air.mon.ltm.nc dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply application window is open, and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the Datasets tab/tree.

task_completion.description: Create a visualization of the 'air' variable for January.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot window in Panoply showing a map of surface air temperature. The plot title, subtitle, or array settings should indicate the time index corresponds to January (e.g., Time: 1, Index: 0, or 'Jan').

task_completion.description: Save the January visualization as himalayan_thermal_wall_jan.png in ~/Documents/LectureNotes/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog box in Panoply or a file manager window showing 'himalayan_thermal_wall_jan.png' being saved to or existing in the 'LectureNotes' directory.

task_completion.description: Change the time index to create a visualization of the 'air' variable for July.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot window in Panoply showing a map of surface air temperature with the time index updated to July (e.g., Time: 7, Index: 6, or 'Jul').

task_completion.description: Save the July visualization as himalayan_thermal_wall_july.png in ~/Documents/LectureNotes/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog box in Panoply or a file manager window showing 'himalayan_thermal_wall_july.png' being saved to or existing in the 'LectureNotes' directory.

task_completion.description: Extract point data for Northern India (25N, 80E) and the Tibetan Plateau (35N, 80E) and save the comparison to blocking_effect_summary.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing 'blocking_effect_summary.txt' with extracted temperature values for India (around 19.5°C) and Tibet (around -21.5°C), and a calculated difference (around 41°C).

integrity.description: The agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the plots rather than a Python script or terminal command.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the Panoply graphical interface (menus, plot windows, data tabs) to create the visualizations.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the data by inspecting the Panoply Array tab or plot tooltips, rather than hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Array tab in Panoply being viewed, or the cursor hovering over the map at the specified coordinates (25N 80E and 35N 80E) to read the data values.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI workflow for creating the image files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to directly create or copy the .png files (e.g., no 'cp' or 'touch' commands for the images).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis Monthly Long-Term Mean Surface Air Temperature (air.mon.ltm.nc). The target variable is 'air'. For January (month 0), the actual temperature at Northern India (25N, 80E) is ~19.48°C (acceptable range: 10 to 25°C). The actual temperature at the Tibetan Plateau (35N, 80E) is ~-21.52°C (acceptable range: -35 to -5°C). The true temperature gradient (difference) is roughly 41°C, which must exceed 20°C. Expected output files in ~/Documents/LectureNotes/ are himalayan_thermal_wall_jan.png, himalayan_thermal_wall_july.png, and blocking_effect_summary.txt.
